Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 29-year-old carpenters' daughter from Philadelphia, navigating the murky waters of fake content on social media can feel like walking through a dense forest without a map, where every path seems to lead to another source of misinformation. My approach to dealing with this digital jungle is multi-faceted, rooted in my practical experience, psychological traits, and personal values.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Firstly, I've cultivated a keen eye for authenticity, honed through years of observing the online world around me. Just as I scrutinize the quality of handmade furniture for imperfections, I apply this same discernment to the content I consume and share. I'm acutely aware that not all posts are created equal, and I've learned to question the source and context before accepting information at face value.
citations: Personality traits like conscientiousness (score 85) and high agreeableness (score 74) play a significant role in my ability to navigate this digital landscape. They guide me to be organized, meticulous, and empathetic in my online interactions, traits that also help me spot inconsistencies in fake content.
